posts.title: Why did I decide to write a blog?
posts.subtitle: Life is a gift. Each of us always has a choice: either destruction, or moving forward.
posts.bodyHtml:
You know, life is a gift, a very special one. It is a gift given to us out of grace. Yet for some foolish reason we don't appreciate it as much as we should. We see the world as a place full of anger and suffering, and we have gotten so used to it that we no longer even try to change it. But it doesn't have to be this way. Each of us always has a choice, in every moment. If you are attentive, you know that there is always a choice: either destruction, or moving forward.

To me, life is a great lesson. We have the chance to see it as a gift and a kind of test from a "great teacher," or as something ordinary and taken for granted, something you can sleep through, drink through, waste on sex or television.

We have the ability to create in life both pain and suffering, as well as compassion and love. My own experiences have taught me that hurting, destroying, taking, being indifferent and selfish, that is all easy. But to love, to move forward, to care for others and forget about yourself, to forgive, to give, and to create joy, that is much more challenging. And for that we need real courage and strength. The strength of a warrior for love and peace, first within oneself, and then in society and in the whole world.

To take responsibility for our actions and look our own demons, cowardice, and arrogance in the eye, and deal with them. They can be everyday habits that bind us like chains, or mental programs and uncontrolled thoughts in our mind. Painful emotions we fear, indifference and laziness that feel easier than leaving our comfort zone. They may seem like harmless little things or our daily routines, but their scent is still that of death, and their goal is pain and suffering. Whether it is suffering we cause ourselves, or suffering we cause others, including nature and animals.

You might live a life full of luxury, but then old age will knock on your door with the final bill, and the invoice for your actions will arrive like an unexpected event. We all feel that there is an order and a law in this world, a law set by life itself. We can call it karma, or divine justice. It's not about being rewarded or punished based on merit, but about the simple truth that every action has its consequence, and nothing happens by accident. The bill comes to everyone, and that is fair. Not as punishment, but as experience.

And that is why I decided to write this blog: so I can publicly share my life story, my insights, and my values, and invite you to a shared communication and connection for the good of our world. To support each other and learn from our collective life stories.

Knowledge, truth, and experience are not found in novels or action movies. I love movie superheroes who protect people from injustice and villains, but they are not going to come. A secret beauty will not appear to make you happy, and a fairy-tale win will not fall into your lap. Life has already begun and time is running. Now is the time to live it correctly.

It only takes one step at a time, each day making a decision that is not selfish but for others. There are so many possibilities to help, groups, projects, communities, you only need to choose. All it takes is to stop listening to the insatiable mind filled with meaningless desires, and step into the heart, where with every beat our life becomes real life, not a wandering through temptations, desires, and pain in superficial and fleeting things, situations, and people.

None of us is perfect, and we don't have to be! Life in the body is not about achieving perfection of the mind, but about achieving the value of love, true compassion, and the right values for life. Those who accept responsibility for their actions, but also surrender them to the Eternal, to God, who do not cling to them and do not claim them egoistically as their own, have the chance to free themselves internally from everything that pulls them down. But those who choose a foolish life, power and fame, or indifference and ignorance, they will not find inner peace. They act blindly, clinging to their illusions, suffering for them, or creating pain, problems, and destruction for others.

Everyone has a choice, it is up to us. And believe me, I too fell all the way to the gates of my own hell, but I chose differently. And today I am truly very happy and free. I love my life and the little things in it. Even great fears like the fear of death or the fear of rejection and judgment by others, are like crumbs on the floor, because my life has been fulfilled. Filled with the knowledge of love for God, the wisdom of creation, and the infinite trust between me and God.

I am ready to accept the new challenges life will prepare for me. I know that the only arms I want to fall into are the arms of unconditional love, the beings of light who are aware of God's presence, and into the arms of God Himself and my Guru.

Just Love.

Om Namo Narayanaya

With heartfelt gratitude. 💛
Romana Anuradha dasi

posts.title: About Happiness
posts.subtitle: Happiness is a choice from within. It must not depend on any cause or result.
posts.bodyHtml:
Hello, I chose the topic of happiness for today's article. I thought I was a relatively happy person, even because I considered superficial actions and deeds to be happiness.

I used to feel happy in fulfilling my ideas and reaching my goals. For example, that I would have this car or that I would have a successful relationship with this and that person. When I reached my goals, fulfilled my ideas, I was a winner and happy. I evaluated my life and my situation as successful and happy, because I achieved what I wanted. When not, I felt failure and anger. This is how I searched for myself and how I was satisfying myself and my feelings of happiness until I realized that it was a delusion, that it was just an illusory notion of happiness.

What do I mean? What delusion?

The illusion that I must first have a goal. That I must achieve something, something distant and only then I could be happy. But then I always have a feeling of lack or failure and I can even suffer because I do not get my idea of happiness. Then I often thought that I was in a phase where I suffer, in a phase where I can't be well, because my happiness is so far away from me. Oh it will be great when I have the car, it will be great when I make friends with this and that person, when I will get there and there.

Another common pattern is that I wait for this and that person to come and he will give me that happiness, he will fill me with happiness, it will not work without him.

When I realized that I was living this pattern, I failed very deep. I felt alone and dependent on almost everything. I realized how this world affects me, how it affects my feeling of happiness. I was slowly finding out how dependent I was on how others treat me, how they react or if they help me fulfill my idea to be finally happy.

I soon realized that happiness is about choosing to be happy NOW. I knew that the only way to be happy was to give up everything internally; to detach oneself from the ideas and feelings that I think are bringing or will bring happiness to me.

Start being happy on your own… so ordinary but so expensive at the same time.

What is happiness?

The feeling of happiness is a state in which I can choose to live, but it is a decision from within and must not depend on any cause or result. It is a state of being in the present moment again and again. It is simple. Sufficient amount of respect, humility and modesty is enough. These qualities are necessary. Because pride and greed never have enough and they will never be satisfied even if they get everything that the mind has determined. Therefore, the control of thoughts and desires is one of the keys to a lasting sense of happiness.

So today I already know that I can't want the feeling of happiness from anyone or anything else than from myself and that I will take full responsibility for my happiness. But not by transferring it to a new vision of myself, but to be happy now.

But who really took responsibility for his happiness? You can argue that it was you who did it, that you have taken responsibility by, for example, that you go to work and then you buy what you need for the money you earn and then you feel happiness. But here's the same formula again, you buy a feeling of happiness, or you buy someone else to fill you with happiness. For example, you buy gifts for your children to make them feel more satisfied, and then it is you who feel more satisfied as well, but in turn you pass on the responsibility for your happiness to your children or to those objects. So we are constantly in the illusory notion of happiness that is constantly distant or dependent on the crazy cycle of the emotional trade.

I know that no one taught us and no one showed us how to be happy. No one told us as a child or as a teenager, "Be happy because you are, you can be happy now." It's about deciding for happiness.

So accepting responsibility for your own happiness is the first step; giving up all ideas about what happiness can bring you and stop grabbing those people and objects, but grabbing happiness itself right away.

You can visualise it in a meditation.

First, meditate on your happiness. My happiness, be a part of me and my journey through life from now on.

Second, control your mind and thoughts. Start using your mind differently. It is not your master; it serves you, it is your instrument. The mind is like fire – it is a good servant but a bad master.

Third, focus on activities that connect you to the feeling of happiness, but don't be attached to them. Attach yourself to that happiness itself.

Can you feel the difference? It is a work in itself and I would like us all to find the true value and meaning of happiness. This is also because we would change a lot of destructive habits in the world. Perhaps we would cease to be emotionally dependent on each other and not be strong, instead we would all be immersed in our sense of happiness, love, abundance. We would become strong pillars of society. The illusion would dissolve. Everyone who discovers his happiness in his heart then radiates it to their surroundings and becomes a real support.

In society, it happens like this. Happiness is something you must work very hard for, something you have to strive for and fight for. It's like a hot live coal game. A live piece of coal is luck and we throw it from one hand to another, no one can hold it, but everyone wants it. And those who don't have it envy those who have it in their hands; and those who are greedy hold the coal so tightly that in the end it burns them. But as you can see, happiness is not a coal. Just stop playing this game, leave the circle of coal players and go to get your happiness in your heart.

Accept situation in your life as it is and decide modestly for happiness. And you who have a difficult life, I pray for you, that through the grace of unconditional love, you will be relieved and you may be accepted by yourself.

Hug another person and tell him, "Be happy too, but not because I'm hugging you, but because you want to!"

Don't take yourself so seriously, be happy with yourself. If you want to know more, write to me. Of course, such a process can take longer. It also depends on our life experience and qualities and values and living conditions. But as I say, don't take yourself so seriously. Because when you are taken seriously, pride and arrogance can come in that prevent us from progressing. It is important to feel free, then you are able to accept. Remember that your true essence is love. And it is our nature to love, giving love and being love. Chant mantras to concentrate your mind. Take responsibility for yourself and be happy, I will try too :-)

Sri Vitthala Giridhari Parabrahmane Namaha ❤️🍀🌞

With Love,
Anuradha Dasi

posts.title: Service
posts.subtitle: Whom do you serve? When you serve love, you receive love. When you serve freedom, you receive freedom.
posts.bodyHtml:
Let me ask you something: what do you have a relationship with in your life? What have you built a relationship with? Whom do you serve? What do you serve? Do you serve your pains, pretences, fear, desires, vanity, addictions, quick satisfaction, and cheap feeling of happiness? Or do you serve something that is permanent and eternal? Something that brings lasting happiness and joy, trust, love, abundance, develops potential, and liberates you?

You see, when you serve something limited, painful, and superficial, you also receive its blessing. If you serve suffering, regret, and sadness, then you also receive their qualities. They give you themselves in the best way they can. What do you expect from illusion, that it will fill you with happiness? That is impossible, it has no capacity for that. Because illusion is not divine, illusion is not unconditional and eternal, it is a deception that you yourself support.

So who is the hypocrite and the fool here? Who fell for it? You? Or those you accuse? You choose for yourself which path you will walk. And because you are reading this article and coincidence does not exist, it means that you decided long ago. You decided to step out of illusion. Because there is cause and effect, and that brought you here, you chose it. Recognition of who you are… you want to be yourself. You have chosen the path of seeing the depth of your soul, which is divine and eternal.

It was not easy for me to accept it either. To accept the fact that we live in deception. I myself grew up in our society, where even at the cost of life we believe that what we have built is real life and the purpose of existence. But the truth is that we serve our untamed mind, which never has enough, which is like flames of destruction we do not control. Every superficial desire combined with the arrogance of our egoistic human nature stands above everything, even at the cost of pain and suffering.

We are slaves to our mind, to all habits and entrenched tracks. Because at first glance it seems real, and especially like a quick solution and satisfaction. Right? That feeling when something irritates you and you quickly satisfy it, cover it, and believe you have solved it, but the truth is that you only postponed it or hid it. The reality is that you voluntarily suffer and only create more misery.

So whom do you serve? Hatred and jealousy disguised as love? Tyranny pretending to be a friend, manipulation disguised as kindness, addiction disguised as happiness, fear disguised as abundance? Where are you and your service? To whom? And why?

Because you do not see the boundary within which we move as human society, the boundary between deception and truth. Many of us swim in a sewer and call it an ocean.

It is easy to walk the path of truth, but it may seem difficult when you are lost in illusion and when you have stepped off the path like many others who also do not see, and you support each other. Why is that? Because we have a relationship with it. And that which we are in relationship with feels closer to us than closeness itself.

But listen, what do we have a relationship with? With fear, sorrow, regret, defeat, resignation, lies, pretence, manipulation, addiction, obsession, indifference, laziness, and I could go on. These are many qualities that come from the dual mind. But what relationship do we have with the reality of being? With love, with lasting happiness without external causes, with our kindness and compassion? Relationship with divine qualities within us, like patience, unity, love, qualities that warm the heart and soothe the soul.

With those we do not have a strong relationship, we do not build it, and definitely not publicly. It seems embarrassing to rejoice in society just like that, to show compassion, to show affection. That is something intimate, something for home. But getting drunk senselessly and creating a scandal is considered normal, and it is done in front of everyone.

What ridiculous values! What do you think? What do you feel?

Then the reality of unconditional love, unity, the reality of the essence of our being, which is the soul, and lasting blissful union with the universe seems absurd to our mind. You say, "I do not understand it, I have no relationship with it." Why? Because you never built one. You always prefer the mind over the heart. Of course you do not know. And that is why it is easier to believe the lie that pretends to be truth.

So now I ask you again, whom do you serve? And whom do you want to serve? And it is very likely that you hold yourself back because the mind wants to serve only what it knows, even at the cost of your being. Mainly so that it does not have to change, transform, or take a step forward and cooperate in fulfilling your true nature. Often self-destruction is closer to it. And many of us, even though we know, still run away. For some reason, we overlook the truth. In the end we tell ourselves that the illusion is comfortable. The mind can get used to anything, but your being suffers. Many people live in the sewer, so why not you? But when you bravely climb out of the sewer, a lake is waiting for you. You have a choice.

Accept our lower qualities as part of you, but do not serve them anymore and do not believe the illusion that they are all there is. Accept them, but do not judge. It is a fact that they exist as a by-product, as a factor of our suffering that arises from separation from reality, simply surviving in illusion.

And therefore I encourage you, since you have come this far and read my words, turn your attention to the highest aspect of love, called Prem. Build a relationship with your own divinity, with qualities worthy of a human being, which are part of God and His creation. Become a servant of unconditional love and not a servant of evil.

Because a human being is meant for service. Every breath you take is service, whether you want it or not. Even when you wake up in the morning and go to the toilet, you serve the needs of your body. Then you make breakfast for the needs of your stomach, and so on, up to serving your family or money. We serve constantly.

But when you become a servant of love, and I do not mean desire or chemistry we often mistake for love, but the kind of love where you forget yourself and are here for others, that is true love. Mother, child, friendship, eternal lovers. Unconditional love, love for love, love between you and God, Prem. Then you will be free because such love is freedom. And when you serve freedom, you receive freedom.

When you wisely understand this, there is only one step left: to detach yourself from habits, mental and emotional patterns we took as our own. The conditioning of society, that we must express the correct emotion and have a mental opinion about everything. And therefore paradoxically, our true nature, lasting bliss, love, wisdom, respect, sharing, compassion seems like a great unknown.

Jump! Jump with me into the lake. Be the truth, because we already are. Let us lift the veil together.

Satguru

Allow me now to touch the topic of Satguru. I know it is the last thing you want to hear. Society has its general idea about them, but even that comes from illusion.

Who is a satguru? When he is a true, real satguru, a teacher of teachers, he never comes for himself. He already has everything and can have anything he wishes, not because he is so perfectly powerful from the perspective of manipulative ego, but because he is fully immersed in the truth we are searching for. And he is absolutely humble toward what he has received, what he carries. He is truth itself; he is what we are searching for. Everything is contained within him. He is aware of reality and illusion. And he came to remind us and help us on our path.

That is why we ask him to guide us, and we are willing to give him everything, everything limited, because what use is it? You know it has no real value. Well, maybe it has some value, but only when you already are yourself. And often the satguru, without asking anything from you, is always close to you, like a father and mother to their child. And when he asks something from you, it is only for your progress toward the Light.

Therefore it is grace when the satguru calls you, he gives you the opportunity to be inspired and to learn.

A being fully immersed in divine reality is God, or rather, God is Him. He let go of everything that prevented him from being That, and he succeeded. And he tells us that we, too, are drops of this ocean, and that it is possible. "I came to remind you, but I cannot do it for you," he often says.

For me, the satguru represents, and is, the highest truth of this creation, which is part of everything. Unity and energy that moves everything, the Source, God, the Universe, Nirvana, Satcitānanda, he is Prem, or rather, they are Him.

The incredible grace and awareness that when you decide to serve Him, it is like serving Truth itself and God. For our mind, this is simple to perform and understand. Not all of us are able to fully comprehend the depth of serving the infinite God, who seems abstract, or serve qualities like bliss, Satcitānanda, which we have never experienced. But the satguru becomes a clear object of service and one-pointed focus for the heart longing for freedom. It is a wonderful opportunity to build a relationship with God through the relationship with the satguru.

For a human being this is natural, and yet rare, so rare that it can be considered God's mercy.

Tell me, how can one deepen a relationship with God, who is infinite, incomprehensible, eternal, unlimited, who is Reality, while we as humans are temporary, limited, understandable, and living in illusion? This is the opportunity when God gives Himself to those who hear, those who see, or at least those who want. Even just thinking of Him immerses you deeper into Truth.

Thanks to the presence of the satguru, the journey of the soul is no longer like waving into empty space, but like being embraced by the divine father and mother who help you grow.

How unconditional can Love be? What is grace like? Do we even understand the meaning of these words?

You know, I would say that unconditional Love is brave, liberating, truthful, tolerant, forgiving, compassionate, and it is grace for the one who ignites it in their heart. And now I ask you again. When someone is fully immersed in these qualities, when someone is these qualities, can they act otherwise? Grace gives only grace, as I wrote at the beginning. You serve humanity, humanity is given to you. You serve your desires, you will desire. You serve lies, you will lie. You serve pleasure, you will be pleased. If you serve illness, it will come with its blessing.

And therefore, all truly saints are those who truly focused on God, if you will, on the truth of Creation, the Source, or the Lord. In Hinduism we have various forms of the Lord with all His mantras and names, like Nārāyana, Vishnu, Krishna, Vittala, Narasimha, or Rama. Saints focused on the highest or deepest experience and knowledge, focused on the reality of God, on bliss and grace itself. They testify to it through their experience. They are saints of this truth and bring us its message, the message of the kingdom of God within our heart after laying aside the veil of ignorance. And believe me, none of them had it easy, hence the glory of God. Glory lined with whip, chains, thorns like the path of Christ. But he also knew that the beauty of the rose goes despite the thorns. And the thorns are human stupidity and ignorance.

And I also ask God for eternal service to Him. Not for myself to become divine, but because there is still the "I" that represents small ego, the seed of arrogance and pride, which leads back to deception. But "I" ask Him that I may serve Him eternally, because He is grace, He is unconditional love and bliss, and thus eternal freedom. When I serve grace, grace is given to me.

So I ask you once again. Whom do you serve? I serve my satguru Paramahamsa Vishwananda. And every day I work so that this relationship through service becomes unity.

Thank you that I can serve you. Amen.

With love and prayer for you,
Romana Anuradha dasi

posts.title: Earth Meditation Movement
posts.subtitle: Four minutes a day to send gratitude and love back to our Mother Earth.
posts.bodyHtml:
I would like to dedicate today's article to the beautiful meditation for Mother Earth and the wonderful Earth Meditation Movement project.

The first thing that caught my attention is that the meditation is for Mother Earth. And that is because it is our planet, the nature that gave us our bodies. It is our common mother. And yet we forget what an important role this mother plays in our lives and our existence. We live here but we give her so little attention, importance and respect. Earth is extremely loving; for centuries, expecting nothing from us and only giving. And we are like naughty children. Now it's time to change that! Because if the children suffer, then their mother suffers with them. If we are not able to change for the sake of ourselves, then we could change for her, for nature and for the other beings that inhabit the planet with us. It is the gift of the incredible diversity of the five elements that has shaped us. Each of us is a unique and beloved child of our planet. And that is why it is so real and direct to name our planet, Mother Earth. If we allow ourselves to be humble and sensitive, we can experience this beautiful reality and become a loving child of this beautiful Planet Earth.

Loving and respecting ourselves and the Earth is a complete stepping stone to a state of happiness and joy. Because it is only when we give love that we are fulfilled; it is our nature. It is sad that out of our foolishness we have built such a society and system where we have forgotten our relationship with our Mother Earth. Out of pride and arrogance we have distanced ourselves from her. Now we can see that this is not the best way to live happily on Earth.

The Earth Meditation Movement is a wonderful project that reminds us that it is absolutely necessary and beautiful to spend at least a few minutes a day with our Earth, our Planet, our Mother, and to send our gratitude and love back to her for all the gifts she offers us. It is only thanks to her that we can build our society. There is not a single thing on this Planet that does not come from her. Everything is created and is constantly offered.

But it won't go on like this indefinitely. I believe that even Planet Earth has its limits. A relationship is always about mutual support, which is why it is so important to offer love and gratitude back to our Earth too. To give back to her the abundance she gives us!

Meditation is one of the thousands of ways we can help, and how we can begin to truly love our Earth again; it is also how we can help society find the right values. Support life, love, health, joy, abundance and meditate with us! Four minutes that will warm your heart through the day, because the person who gives, will receive it back. He who loves, is loved. After all, we do not always have to be mindful of scarcity, anger and power… Let us turn our attention to qualities such as respect, reverence, help, hope, goodness and justice. And the first step can be the Earth Meditation.

Another thing that I am very interested in is the goals of the Earth Meditation Movement. And that is, for example, to teach meditation to children (to teach it to a new generation), to use it at concerts, at family barbecues, at the opening of meetings and gatherings, in companies or schools, before courses or trainings, and other common occasions.

Actually, I like trying to squeeze those lovely 4 minutes of our time wherever we can. This short meditation helps to naturally place the right values and priorities in a society that is moving towards abundance. It is a small gesture that contains a big message. A message of love, humility, unity, patience and faith, leading to happiness, health and peace.

The meditation shows us that loving is easy and most importantly, very beautiful. And it can start now and everywhere!

Another goal of the Earth Meditation Movement is to reach one million practitioners, and to create a worldwide Earth Meditation Movement community to join forces and create unity for change in the world while supporting planet Earth.

So, let's love our planet, its diversity and love.

Thank you again and again and I wish you many successful achievements.

Love,
Romana

worldmovement.org

posts.title: Live in harmony with nature
posts.subtitle: The story of how I began to live in harmony with ecology, nature and a clear conscience.
posts.bodyHtml:
Hello, my name is Anuradha dasi and I would like to share with you the story of how I began to live in harmony with ecology, nature and a clear conscience.

At the age of 16, I started studying both, conventional and organic farming at the Agricultural Academy. Here I learned how conventional farming works, with chemicals for rapid production of animal products, including animals itself.

I really wanted to have my own farm and do everything differently, correctly, naturally. After few years I started living with my ex boyfriend from Germany. He had a family farm in a beautiful part of Germany, but he did not want to join organic farming and neither did his family. I wasn't angry, but I left him a few years later.

I've found that if I wanted to help in that Ecological direction, I would need to make small steps starting with myself. First, I stopped eating meat. Another big step to live a healthy lifestyle, was to stop giving toxin to my body, I stopped smoking cigarettes and drinking alcohol. To live in harmony with nature is possible by taking small steps.

A person is much more vital, less tired, and more connected to oneself. I also started to look better right away. Cooking is fun. I felt joy, more concentration and satisfaction. In a few months, I added regular meditation and yoga in my routine. In a few months, I was ready for a new stage of life.

I traveled to Germany and lived for a year in the "vegan" Ashram of Shree Peetha Nilaya, Springen. I volunteered to cook in a large kitchen, with the best vegetables goods from Mother Earth. I recognized the huge gift of been involved in the new production of "eco" food. In this way, I am anchored in veganism and ecological principles of life.

After I left Ashram, I started working as a cook in an Asian organic vegan kitchen and learned a lot of tasty recipes. I have also decided, to live a life without plastic bags and disposable plastics. I always carry a bag for fruit and vegetables, a glass bottle with water and a travel cup for coffee. I'm constantly trying my best for us and our planet Earth.

Today I live and work, in an organic walnut farm. We sell not only nuts, but also different fruits and products. (2022)

When, 6 years ago, after breaking up with a friend, I decided to follow the path of the heart, I had no idea how many beautiful experiences, associated with ecology, respect, love and the wisdom of nature awaited me.

Also, I had enough courage and freshness for occasional foto modeling, for like minded people I feel the same way. Photography connected with nature and the 5 elements.

And probably the most beautiful experiences in harmony with Mother Nature came during the time with the Rainbow community and its movement… a community that has gatherings all over the world and lives according to the ancient traditions of the rainbow tribe of Indians and shamans.

I am grateful to have great healthy food on the table and money to purchase organic food and cosmetik. As if the whole universe was behind me to support me in my decision to live happily but with respect for the planet.

My Gurudev Paramahamsa Vishwananda constantly remind us to plant trees and to love nature and animals.

Thank you for this gift in life and I wish you many right decisions.

PS: And those words that are not kind and are directed at him from me, when I am angry, when I don't understand, when I am in darkness and ignorance, when I cry out for help and I am in pain, they hurt me terribly afterward. But in the end, I believe I can forgive myself, and that he is not angry with me. I thank him deeply for being able to encourage me like that and show me that love and that true path.

I know I cannot do this on my own. But I have the will to want to change, step by step to be a better person and give more space to my soul, to the divinity in my life, and especially to Guru. So that all of this comes first and only then me.

And to manage those difficult things, early rising, discipline and so on. It's not easy, but it's worth it. I can be joyful in all of this. That is my most heartfelt conviction, because all glory belongs to the Satguru, who holds us in his arms and by the hand.

Thank you.

Anuradha dasi ❤️🍀🌞

posts.title: What Preceded the Gītā?
posts.subtitle: The Mahābhārata, the battle of Kurukṣetra, and the inner war we each must fight.
posts.bodyHtml:
Jai Gurudev, for this essay I chose the first topic: "What events preceded the Gītā?"

Since the Gītā is part of a much larger work, the Mahābhārata, I will first describe the Mahābhārata. It is an ancient Indian epic. The name Mahābhārata means: Mahā = great and Bhārata refers to ancient India. The authorship is attributed to Vyāsa.

The Mahābhārata tells the story of two dynasties the Pāṇḍavas and the Kauravas. Let's say that the founders of these lineages were two brothers. One was Pāṇḍu, who had five sons: Yudhiṣṭhira, Arjuna, Bhīma, Sahadeva, and Nakula. The other brother was Dhṛtarāṣṭra, who had 100 sons, the eldest of whom was Duryodhana.

There was tension between the sons of Pāṇḍu and Dhṛtarāṣṭra from childhood. They grew up together as cousins and shared the same martial arts teacher, their guru Droṇa. Nevertheless, the Kauravas constantly put pressure on the Pāṇḍavas, inciting rivalry that escalated to attempts to kill them. After the death of Pāṇḍu, the blind king Dhṛtarāṣṭra divided the rule between his sons and Pāṇḍu's sons. It's important to note that the Pāṇḍavas would have been willing to rule peacefully alongside the Kauravas. However, led by Duryodhana, the Kauravas deceitfully stole their kingdom and exiled them for 13 years.

In the meantime, the Pāṇḍavas married Princess Draupadī. Acting with love and righteousness, they helped others wherever they went. Their great friend was Lord Krishna, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, who always supported the Pāṇḍavas. The Mahābhārata contains many stories and līlās (divine pastimes) involving the Pāṇḍavas, Draupadī, and Krishna.

Some of the worst deeds the Kauravas committed against the Pāṇḍavas include: building a palace made of flammable lacquer to burn them alive with their mother Kuntī; attempting to poison Bhīma; and tricking them in a game of dice to rob them of everything.

I would also like to mention the figure of Bhīṣma, who symbolizes our ego. He was the grandfather of both dynasties and had vowed to always protect the throne of Hastināpura. But he had not anticipated that Duryodhana would seize power supported by his brothers. Though Bhīṣma was well aware that Duryodhana embodied pride and his brothers other negative qualities, he upheld his vow and remained loyal to the throne, even in the approaching war. This was difficult for him because he loved the Pāṇḍavas.

Duryodhana's behavior was selfish and greedy, driven by anger and desire. His father, King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, was blind, a symbol of our own blind mind. The Pāṇḍavas represent our good qualities, supported by Krishna.

When the exile ended, Duryodhana was supposed to return the kingdom to the Pāṇḍavas, but he refused. The conflict escalated into a great war. An important moment came when both Arjuna and Duryodhana approached Krishna to request His support. Krishna gave them a choice: either His vast army or Himself, unarmed. Arjuna, sitting humbly at Krishna's feet, chose Krishna as his charioteer, gladly giving up the army. Duryodhana, overjoyed with Arjuna's choice, took the army and believed himself unbeatable.

The battle took place at Kurukṣetra. On one side stood the Pāṇḍavas, and on the other the Kauravas. Before the battle began, Arjuna asked Krishna to drive his chariot between the two armies and this is where the Bhagavad Gītā begins.

Bhagavad Gītā and its Revelation

The Bhagavad Gītā is different from the Mahābhārata in that it is śruti, what is heard or divinely revealed, in this case directly from Krishna, the Supreme Lord. The Mahābhārata is smṛti, what has been written down.

Arjuna is horrified when he sees the Kauravas: his relatives, teachers, and friends. Despite all the wrong they did to him and his brothers, he feels compassion and refuses to fight. Thus begins a dialogue between Krishna and Arjuna, resulting in one of the most important scriptures in Hinduism, perhaps even the world, the Bhagavad Gītā ("Song of the Supreme"). Krishna imparts transcendental wisdom to Arjuna, offering a timeless guide to life for all of us.

Reflection

I would like to reflect on Arjuna's behavior at the start of the Gītā. Despite all that happened, he still doesn't want to fight. This is surprising because he was an experienced warrior, familiar with battles. Considering everything the Kauravas had done to them, his hesitation is shocking. But when I look at human life, I see that we too often fall into the same doubt. We may know something is harmful, yet blindly let it continue instead of confronting it.

Conclusion

When we reflect on what the Mahābhārata and the events leading to the Bhagavad Gītā show us, it becomes clear that we ourselves are participants in the battle of Kurukṣetra, within our own lives. It's up to us which side we take: Will we attach ourselves like Droṇa, support pride like Duryodhana, or blindly watch wrongdoings like Dhṛtarāṣṭra? Will we stubbornly cling to our ego like Bhīṣma? Or will we, like the sons of Pāṇḍu, blow our conch shells and confront the army of our lower qualities, just as Arjuna ultimately did, representing our soul's fight to win this inner war and claim the rightful kingdom of self-mastery?

Because where Krishna stands, there is always victory.

Anuradha Dāsī

posts.title: OM Chanting & Project Mantra
posts.subtitle: Two free practices to quiet the mind, open the heart, and share love for Mother Earth.
posts.bodyHtml:
OM Chanting

OM Chanting is a free group practice that uses the transformational power of OM to activate the self-healing potential of participants. The practice transforms negativity into positive energy, helps purify the local environment, and supports the physical, emotional and spiritual well-being of participants.

Love is meant to be shared. OM Chanting brings people together to share the positive vibrations of OM and their love for Mother Earth in local communities and nations around the world. In this way, regular OM Chanting circles and monthly Full Moon circles are a priceless gift for humanity.

Experience the Power of OM

Enhance Overall Well-Being

The practice promotes inner peace, mental clarity, emotional freedom and physical health. Many experience positive shifts which allow them to enjoy more peace, joy and love in their lives.

Transform Negativity into Positive Energy

Due to a special blessing, OM Chanting generates a vibrational frequency that releases negativity, transforms it and showers participants with positive energy.

Purify the Environment

The vibrational waves created by the circle spread out in a 2-kilometre radius, creating a harmonious, peaceful environment and a unity between humans and nature. The OM continues to resonate in participants' bodies after the circle, allowing them to share the good vibrations wherever they go.

Grace of the Masters

Many people are aware of the power of OM, but only a Master knows how to activate its full potential. Paramahamsa Vishwananda is such a Master. Through his blessing and grace, this ancient practice is available to help create positive shifts around the world.

Listen to live OM Chanting on SoundCloud

Project Mantra

Project Mantra is a free, guided program that helps you gradually build up an easy, yet powerful daily chanting practice over 40 days. It is perfect for beginners who want a simple, effective tool to quiet the mind, open the heart, and create a positive habit of connecting with the Divine on a daily basis.

Project Mantra is our mantra meditation practice. Everyone can do it, anytime and anywhere, even children. Simple daily repetitions of a mantra will take you deep into the beauty and mystery of chanting the Divine Name. The practice is especially powerful when you turn every repetition into a heartfelt prayer.

Simple and Powerful

Redirect the Mind

Mantra chanting is an effective way to control a restless mind. Through regular repetition, the mind can be trained to focus on something positive – like the Divine Names – rather than the incessant inner judgments that tend to occupy our thoughts. Used for millennia, by every spiritual tradition, mantras are the predecessor of modern day affirmations.

Open the Heart

The power of a mantra is not hidden in its meaning, but found in its vibrational frequency. The most powerful mantras are timeless Sanskrit names for the Divine. Each syllable creates a vibration that awakens and manifests a Divine Quality such as peace, courage, love, compassion, and acceptance. The secret is to chant with love and devotion as this opens the heart, amplifies the vibration and allows you to experience Divine Love.

Develop a Positive Habit

It takes 40 days for the transformational power of the practice to create a permanent shift in your consciousness. During that time, you may also notice that you have a more positive outlook on life, improved concentration, greater self-awareness and less judgment toward yourself and others.

Anuradha dasi 🍀❤️🌞

posts.title: A New Dimension
posts.subtitle: An essay on spirituality, coaching, and how the inner world shapes our everyday reality.
posts.bodyHtml:
I have decided to publish the essay I have written during my credited coaching exams (2017).

A New Dimension

My essay is focused on spirituality and how it is increasingly permeating everyday lives of increasing number of people in today world. Great number of successful individuals are connected to the source of spiritual perception of the world and they are aware that it is the foundation of healthy success and lasting happiness in life. At the beginning I would also like to delve into meaning of the word spirituality. Spirituality is when one understands that events in material world first manifest in thought, idea, inspiration, longing, vibration, intuition, mood, in imagination or in Gods voice – in other words in non-material world. That is how I know that events in material world originate inside me and then they manifest in the outside. I know that what is inside me later becomes my outside reality. As they say: "both in heaven and on earth". Spirituality has no God, or rituals, or any religion. To be spiritual means that I perceive the spiritual plane of the world. That is what great masters, yogis and avatars always thought us. Only later did people create religions, sects or churches.

My complicated and difficult life experiences brought me onto the path of realisation that human life is not only about consumeristic perception of the world, when you see yourself as a body with senses, but that there is a lot more inside me, and that is my true self. That there is outreaching, unified, all pervading stream of eternity that is always present.

For our whole life, we are growing with false image of the world. From very young age we are told whom we should aspire to be, how we should and should not behave, how to survive in the artificial world build upon system of fear and material perception. Even I rather ran away, not only with the help of drugs, into my own world, where I, after many great and un-comprehensible down-falls, found that something that could be described as a new breath, discovering yourself or rebirth.

I have realised that human society goes hand in hand with biological, scientific, technical and also spiritual evolution. I have found intuitive guidance within myself. It teaches me how to live at all the levels and how to be stable, satisfied and consistently happy and enriching human being. In other words, it is return to human nature and to humbleness in times of development. Many have forgotten that everything is connected and that everything flows together in one stream. All natural cycles, all inhabitants of the planet are part of this stream. When Human steps into this stream, they become happier, more natural, more successful, more enriching, more stable, healthier, in general their whole live changes for the better. To be more precise, in reality we are all part of the rhythm of universe and this surreally large intelligence. We either dance with it consciously and keep the pace, or we are getting tangled into ideas about life, that originate in mind and are therefore limited, since mind is only small part of the entirety. All that is created by mind therefore brings inevitable doom. On the other hand, all that comes through true knowledge is eternal in nature.

There are a lot of various guides and terminology about how to be more positive, to be happier etc. Experienced and wise humans know that everything comes and originates from this spiritual plane. They start to explore it more deeply and find out how the inner attunement positively impacts their reality. Then the spirituality and use of things supporting spirituality (such as meditation, observing one self and yoga) become part of everyday life. In this sense we can perceive massive shift in the world and within individuals.

Nonetheless majority of population lives in inner fear. Fear brings destruction and people that are afraid are easy to manipulate. This can be advantageous to large corporations. When we perceive the world through the veil of fear (feeling deprivation, fighting for position, feeling threatened), you naturally become selfish, power hungry, greedy and indifferent towards others. We can change this by incorporating positive elements into our everyday lives. Each one of us starts to change for the better from the inside and starts to create new material reality for ourselves and the world.

Modern psychology says: We acknowledge our mistake. In the past, we were not trying to find a solution to how to guide normal humans to happiness and how to keep them in healthy psychical condition, by which we could prevent severe mental breakdowns or conditions. It is also scientifically proven that long lasting feeling of happiness and stability is impacted by honest service to others, living in present moment and for the given activity, also design of things and surroundings are important. Positive psychology is already applying results of its research, which I see as a major advancement. Alas everything is already written in in ancient scriptures and literary works of our world, that are based on spirituality. It is important to remember that all these works are written in words of their time.

Each day more and more people realise that todays world has its limits and they are asking themselves how to change it. All of us who asked this question answer: realise and change yourself. This process of change is very complex and for some even painful. We often need help of the others, that is why many coaches, therapists, masters or centres start to lean towards alternative solutions.

My wish is to help people who are feeling the need for change in live, and even help to those, who are still lost in labyrinths of unawareness and inevitably arrive to the limits of their power or got lost in dead ends. To help by gentle yet effective methods such as coaching and therapy and to point them towards their goals or changes in life. I found my inspiration in, for example Mr. Jelínek who is coach and uses meditation and works with energy channels and chakras in our body. I found out that he also uses discoveries in quantum physics, for example the fact that every atom is in its core composed of energy, therefore the whole material world (air, water, minerals, plants, animals and humans) are composed of energy. When we change this energy inside us and stabilize it at the highest frequency, we will perceive it as happiness, unconditional love, or bliss. This automatically opens inside us the path towards full potential of our being. It is possible to measure this vibration. The studies on this subject were conducted by doc. Masaru Emoto. High frequency energies enhance our creativity, expand our world view and self-confidence. On the other hand, low frequency energies cause fear, limit our world view, keep us in old programs and routines, support our indifference towards life, nature and other people. I was also inspired by other personalities, such as Albert Einstein, Michael Jackson, princes Diana, Jaroslav Dušek, Marek Dzirasa, Mahatma Gandhi a Paramahamsa Vishvananda.

Everyday I wish that those that live in inauspicious parts of the world that are ravaged by war, natural disasters or rule by dictators, will be once able to live in as much freedom as we live in now. And I wish for us living in better environment to stop creating illusory fears and hostile atmosphere. I believe that people will soon realise that deficiency does not exist, when we are ourselves and live in love and truth. Those are the laws of nature and universe that are stronger than human mind or intellect. To live in heart is easy and natural, you just need to accept that mind is only one of the human tools, same as body, feelings or techniques, and not foundation for building of human society.

Romana Ezrová Anuradha dasi

posts.title: My First Darshan
posts.subtitle: The moment that changed my life.
posts.bodyHtml:
My name is Romana Ezrová / Anuradha Dasi, and I would like to share my first darshan experience, which took place in 2015 at Shree Peetha Nilaya in Springen, a meeting that changed my life forever.

For a long time, I felt an emptiness inside and yearned for someone who would truly understand me. I was seeking understanding and acceptance, but the relationships I experienced lacked real depth, trust, and stability. They were not relationships where I could truly be myself. At the same time, I felt like I didn't even understand myself. I didn't know how to live my own life, who I truly was, or what I was searching for. This uncertainty weighed on me every day and brought stress. I longed for answers but didn't know where to find them.

When I first heard about Paramahamsa Vishwananda, I felt that meeting Him could be interesting. I didn't expect anything specific, I just felt drawn to Him. Together with a few other women, we set out on a journey filled with laughter and joyful sharing.

But destiny had a surprise for us. On the way, our car broke down, and we arrived too late, the darshan had already ended. We were disappointed. But everything turned out beautifully. Guruji decided to hold another darshan the next day, and we were given a second chance.

When I entered the room, I felt a special energy. The atmosphere was full of anticipation and love. And then came the moment. When I stepped in front of Gurudev, He looked into my eyes and I felt as though He could see straight into my soul. It was as if He knew everything I had ever felt, joy, sorrow, doubt, the longing to be accepted. And above all, He showed me something I had never experienced before, unconditional divine, sweet love!

It was the moment I realised that true love places no conditions, it doesn't judge, it doesn't measure. It simply is. And Paramahamsa Vishwananda not only teaches this, He lives it. In every glance, every word, every encounter.

When He asked me where I came from, and I replied "the Czech Republic," I felt an unexpected peace. Suddenly, everything made sense. I was exactly where I was meant to be.

After the darshan, I left changed. I no longer felt lonely, instead, I felt deep peace and the awareness that I was accepted just as I am. For the first time, I understood that I don't need to seek validation from others, everything I need is already within me.

Since then, I have been attending darshans regularly. Each one teaches me something new, humility, love, gratitude. Guruji has never judged me. He always guided me to grow and become the best version of myself.

I am grateful for every new day I now live and for the opportunity to follow the example of a realised Master and teacher such as Paramahamsa Vishwananda.

Anuradha Dasi

posts.title: Teaching Assistant & Julinka
posts.subtitle: A bond beyond words.
posts.bodyHtml:
My name is Romana Ezrová, and I work as a teaching assistant, supporting children with special needs, especially those on the autism spectrum.

Several years ago, I was invited to help at a summer day camp for children with autism. I had no experience, and honestly, I was nervous. I didn't know what to expect. But from the very first day, I felt a change. These children, although different in how they perceive the world, touched my heart deeply.

I was assigned to a girl named Julinka. From the beginning, there was a special connection between us full of trust, safety, and mutual joy. What started as a professional responsibility grew into a deep and heartfelt bond. Today, I am her personal assistant, and we've been together for over three years.

Our relationship goes beyond the standard assistant-child dynamic. We are like soul friends, even like sisters. This closeness allows me to support her in a natural, intuitive way, filled with love, creativity, and laughter.

Julinka has taught me patience, presence, and the beauty of slowing down. She reminds me to see the world through the eyes of wonder. Time spent with her is never just work, it's meditation, joy, and play in the purest sense. Whether we sing, go on walks, play music, or explore nature, I'm always inspired by her sensitivity and unique perspective.

I am also deeply thankful to her family, who welcomed me with open arms and gave me the trust to walk this path with their daughter. We've become a team working together for her well-being and growth.

Looking back, I could never have imagined how profoundly this role would change me. I didn't just find a job, I found purpose, connection, and a dear friend.

I'm grateful for every moment with Julinka and for the opportunity to be part of her journey.

Romana Ezrová
Teaching Assistant & Personal Support Worker

posts.title: Když se změna neděje silou, ale návratem
posts.subtitle: Jsme zvyklí tlačit na změnu. Mít plán, výkon, výsledky. Ale vnitřní proměna takhle nefunguje.
posts.bodyHtml:
Jsme zvyklí tlačit na změnu. Mít plán, výkon, výsledky. Ale vnitřní proměna takhle nefunguje.

To, co se v nás opravdu mění, se neděje v napětí, ale ve chvíli, kdy se tělo přestane bránit. Když se nervový systém uvolní. Když přestaneme bojovat s tím, co cítíme.

Návrat k sobě není krok vpřed.

Je to spíš zastavení toho, co nás od sebe vzdaluje.

A právě v tom prostoru začíná něco nového. Ne proto, že jsme to vymysleli, ale proto, že to konečně může projít skrz nás bez odporu.

V Beyond Limits nepracujeme s tlakem.

Pracujeme s přítomností.

S tím, co je skutečné právě teď.

S dechem, který se zpomalí.

S tělem, které se uvolní.

S myslí, která nemusí všechno řídit.

A v tom se začíná měnit celý vnitřní svět.

posts.title: Welcome to Beyond Limits
posts.subtitle: A new home for my writings, services, and the 40-day journey. Thank you for being here.
posts.bodyHtml:
Hello, beautiful soul. My name is Romana — Anuradha. I'm so happy you're reading this.

For a long time my work has lived in different places — on my blog at anuradha.blog, on Instagram, in conversations, in retreats. This site, Beyond Limits, is my attempt to bring it all together.

I'm extremely happy to welcome you all on my new blog and website.

I hope you will spend a nice moment reading these pages and will take inspiration with you.

Why "Beyond Limits"

The name carries the whole story. Beyond the limits we were given as children. Beyond the limits society places on identity, on gender, on who is allowed to seek the divine.

Love is not what you want, but who you are.

My older writings also live on at anuradha.blog — I'll keep posting there too. This space will hold my newest thoughts as Beyond Limits grows.

With love,
Romana Anuradha 🍀
